steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage argues that to gain a lasting place in historical memory, “there is little that can ______ being the first to do something.” The example of Jeanne Baré (first woman to circumnavigate the world) confirms that being first is almost impossible to top. The blank must mean something like “be superior to” or “beat out.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'overreach by': means to go too far in pursuit of something. The sentence would say “little can overreach by being first” — grammatically awkward and logically off. ✗
  • Choice B — 'prevail over': means to be superior to or triumph over. “Little can prevail over being first” means almost nothing surpasses the value of being first — exactly what the passage argues. ✓
  • Choice C — 'fluctuate with': means to vary alongside. This has no logical connection to the claim that being first secures historical memory. ✗
  • Choice D — 'constrain within': means to limit or restrict inside something. This does not fit the meaning of surpassing or outweighing being first. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
